title: cystopteris fragilis subsp. regia
intro: Cystopteris fragilis subsp. regia, also known as Royal Lady Fern, is a subspecies of the Cystopteris fragilis plant species. It is a type of fern with delicate, lacy fronds that grow in damp, shaded areas. The medicinal properties of the Royal Lady Fern include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, as well as to promote overall health and well-being.
